title: helosciadium nodiflorum subsp. repens
intro: Helosciadium nodiflorum subsp. repens, also known as creeping meadow-parsley, is a plant species within the carrot family, native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of creeping meadow-parsley include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Helosciadium nodiflorum subsp. repens typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the plant's leaves and roots, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
